headline: How Erdogan's regime built a crony capitalist system in Turkey
description: In less than a quarter of a century, the Islamist-conservative party of Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan handed over the keys of the country's economy to a small circle drawn from a loyal business class and clientelist networks.

headline: Child obesity: Chile's successful experience with warning signs and banned marketing
Iran says deal with US 'has never been closer' as Trump lashes out
Musk becomes world's first trillionaire as SpaceX shares soar
description: After the biggest initial public offering in history, shares in Elon Musk's SpaceX closed 19% higher on their first day of trading. Despite operating at a loss, the company has convinced investors to buy into Musk's ambitious plans.

headline: France bids farewell to Lyhanna, 11, whose killing sparked outrage
description: The body of the young girl was found last week after she went missing on May 29 in the southwestern town of Fleurance.

headline: Europeans' trust in the US hits historic low, poll finds
description: One week ahead of the G7 meeting, a pan-European survey by the European Council on Foreign Relations reveals that Europeans agree on the need to take greater responsibility for their own security, but do not want a divorce from NATO.

headline: Josh O'Connor, star of Spielberg's 'Disclosure Day,' wants to 'cultivate his garden'
description: At 36, he is one of the most sought-after actors in both independent cinema and major Hollywood productions. In just a few years, the British artist, who is passionate about gardening, has put his delicate masculinity and subtle acting style to work for a wide range of characters, from a lovesick farmer to Prince Charles or a priest.

The murky ties between FIFA and the US
description: After the 2022 World Cup was awarded to Qatar, the US launched a legal offensive against several FIFA members before securing the co-hosting of the 2026 tournament. This is the story of the rise of American soft power in global football.

headline: Cocaine transporters, 'narcos' before their time, face trial after 15-year investigation
description: France is trying one of the country's oldest major cocaine trafficking cases, dating back to 2011 and involving smuggling between Guadeloupe and mainland France. The alleged ringleader has been identified and apprehended in Panama after years on the run.

headline: 'Operation Pushkin': Rare books, Georgian con artists and the shadow of the Kremlin
description: For two years, Georgian criminals orchestrated the theft of rare editions of works by Alexander Pushkin from major European libraries. The trial of five of them begins in Paris on June 9. It could reveal whether Russia was involved.
